case_1.quote: An international trading site for VDEMA LTD — covering commodity listings, warehouse capacity, and buyer enquiries across the US, Europe and Africa.

case_2.quote: Jemo.cm — our in-house e-commerce platform positioned as Cameroon's trusted shop, built around Mobile Money checkout and local delivery from day one.

case_3.quote: A full lounge management system for Beverly's Lounge in Bonamoussadi — orders, stock and staff rotations unified where there used to be three notebooks.

process.subtitle: One team from the first call to the quarterly review — no agency-to-freelancer shuffle.

step_1.title: Discover
step_1.description: Workshop your goals, users and constraints. Audit what you already run.

step_2.title: Design
step_2.description: High-fidelity prototypes you can click before a line of code is written.

step_3.title: Build
step_3.description: Ship in fortnightly slices. Real product you can use — not milestones on a chart.

step_4.title: Grow
step_4.description: Iterate on real usage. Monthly metrics review. We stick around past launch.

testimonial_1.quote: BridgeYou delivered a site that speaks to international buyers — clean, professional, and built to handle commodity trading across three continents. They understood our scale.
testimonial_1.author: Van Donald Antiero

testimonial_2.quote: They built Primeselar from the ground up. The platform now handles our entire digital services catalog — subscriptions, streaming, virtual numbers — all paid with MTN and Orange Money.
testimonial_2.author: Nkenfack Teddy

testimonial_3.quote: Our orders, stock and staff shifts used to live in three different notebooks. BridgeYou put everything in one system — we run the lounge with two fewer headaches every night.
testimonial_3.author: Beverly's Lounge team

cta_banner.subtitle: 30-minute call, no obligation. We'll walk through your goals, your stack, and what a pilot could look like.

q1.question: How long until we go live?
q1.answer: Most websites ship in 3–4 weeks. Booking systems and CRMs take 6–10 weeks depending on integrations. We break the work into fortnightly slices you can review and sign off.

q2.question: Do you accept Mobile Money payments?
q2.answer: Yes. MTN MoMo, Orange Money and card payments all work out of the box. We also integrate with your bank if you need direct settlement.

q3.question: What happens after launch?
q3.answer: Every project includes 30 days of support and a handover doc. After that, you can stay on a monthly growth retainer or pay-as-you-go for updates.

q4.question: Can you work in both English and French?
q4.answer: Yes — all of our sites, systems and support are fully bilingual. We write copy in both languages from day one.

q5.question: Do you build for industries outside Cameroon?
q5.answer: We work with teams across Africa and globally. Our base is in Cameroon but the stack, process and Mobile Money integrations transfer across the continent.

q6.question: How do you price projects?
q6.answer: Fixed-price for scoped work, monthly retainer for ongoing growth. We walk you through a clear estimate after the first call — no surprises later.

company.page_subtitle: We're a Douala-based studio serving clients across Cameroon and beyond — building the tools we wish existed, tuned for how businesses on the continent actually operate.
company.registered_intro: BridgeYou Innovations SARL is officially registered in Cameroon. We issue proper invoices, operate under a formal tax regime, and our incorporation documents are available on request.
company.mission_text: We build software the way we'd want it built for us — simple, honest, and measured by real outcomes. We're partners, not vendors. And we're still here after launch.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: BridgeYou Innovations SARL ("BridgeYou", "we", "us") respects your privacy. This policy explains what we collect when you visit bridgeyou.cm or engage us for a project, how we use that information, and the rights you have over it.

sections.heading: 1. Information we collect
sections.body:
When you contact us through the website form or WhatsApp we collect: your name, company, email, phone number, the type of service you're interested in, your indicative budget, and the message you send.

When you simply browse bridgeyou.cm we collect anonymous usage information such as pages visited, approximate location derived from your IP, device type and referrer, through standard server logs and — where enabled — privacy-friendly analytics.

sections.heading: 2. How we use it
sections.body: We use the information you give us to reply to your enquiry, scope and deliver projects you engage us for, send project-related updates, and improve our services. We do not sell or rent your personal information. We do not use it for automated advertising targeting.

sections.heading: 3. Who sees it
sections.body: Access is limited to BridgeYou's founder and, where strictly necessary, a small number of vetted subcontractors bound by confidentiality. Form submissions are processed by Netlify (our hosting platform) and delivered to our business inboxes at bridgeyoucm@gmail.com and the founder's email.

sections.heading: 4. How long we keep it
sections.body: Enquiry and project correspondence is kept while the commercial relationship is active and for up to 5 years after, which is the standard business-record retention period in Cameroon. Anonymous analytics data is retained for up to 14 months.

sections.heading: 5. Your rights
sections.body: You can ask us at any time to: see what data we hold about you, correct it, or delete it. We will honour the request within 30 days unless we're legally required to retain specific records.

sections.heading: 6. Cookies
sections.body: We use strictly necessary cookies to make the site work (language preference, navigation). We do not set third-party tracking or advertising cookies.

sections.heading: 7. Changes to this policy
sections.body: If we materially change how we handle your data we'll update the date at the top of this page and, where appropriate, notify existing clients by email.

privacy.contact_body: Write to bridgeyoucm@gmail.com or use the contact form — we respond within 24 hours.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms apply to services provided by BridgeYou Innovations SARL (RCCM CM-DLA-02-2025-B-13-00674, Douala, Cameroon) to its clients, and to your use of bridgeyou.cm. By engaging us or browsing the site you accept these terms.

sections.heading: 1. Services we offer
sections.body: BridgeYou builds custom websites, booking systems, CRMs, e-commerce platforms, workflow automations, and manages Google / Facebook ad campaigns. The precise scope, deliverables, timeline and pricing for any project are defined in a written proposal or contract signed by both parties.

sections.heading: 2. Engagement & proposals
sections.body: Our website is an introduction — not a binding offer. A project only begins once we have a signed proposal (email acceptance or countersigned PDF) and the agreed deposit is received.

sections.heading: 3. Pricing, invoicing and payment
sections.body: Prices are quoted in FCFA unless otherwise agreed. Fixed-price projects are typically billed 50% at kick-off and 50% at delivery; retainers are billed monthly in advance. Invoices are due within 15 days of issue. Late payments may suspend ongoing work until the balance is settled.

sections.heading: 4. Intellectual property
sections.body: Once final payment is received, the client owns the source code, brand assets and content that BridgeYou has produced specifically for them. BridgeYou retains rights to reusable components, internal libraries, frameworks and know-how used to build the deliverable. Third-party tools (fonts, libraries, payment APIs) remain under their own licences.

sections.heading: 5. Confidentiality
sections.body: Both parties treat information exchanged during an engagement — business plans, customer data, credentials, strategy — as confidential and will not share it with third parties without consent.

sections.heading: 6. Warranty and support
sections.body: Every project includes 30 days of free bug-fix support after launch. After that, maintenance and new features are billed hourly or under a monthly retainer. We do not warrant uninterrupted third-party services (hosting providers, payment processors, APIs).

sections.heading: 7. Limitation of liability
sections.body: To the extent allowed by law, BridgeYou's total liability for any project is limited to the amount paid by the client for that specific project in the preceding 12 months. We are not liable for indirect, consequential or lost-profit damages.

sections.heading: 8. Termination
sections.body: Either party may end a project or retainer with 30 days' written notice. The client pays for work completed up to the termination date, and BridgeYou hands over all completed deliverables.

sections.heading: 9. Governing law
sections.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Cameroon. Any dispute that cannot be resolved amicably will be settled by the competent courts of Douala.

terms.contact_body: Write to bridgeyoucm@gmail.com or use the contact form and we will clarify before you engage.
